presentation-quality.requirement: Maintains professional academic guidance tone without promotional language or casual phrasing
presentation-quality.explanation: The response uses a formal, structured report format with neutral academic wording (e.g., “eligibility criteria,” “required documents,” “selection criteria”) and avoids salesy or casual expressions. Guidance is framed as advisory (“Applicants should verify…”) rather than promotional marketing.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses hierarchical organization with clear sections separating program admission from scholarship information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is clearly structured with numbered top-level sections, including a dedicated section for “Master's in Economics … — Admission Requirements and Eligibility” and a separate dedicated section for “France Excellence Charpak Master's Scholarship — Eligibility and Application Requirements.” This hierarchy cleanly separates program admission content from scholarship information.

presentation-quality.requirement: Formats requirements, documents, and procedures as lists or bullet points for clarity
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeatedly formats requirements, eligibility criteria, documents, and procedures as numbered lists, bullet points, and step-by-step lists (e.g., required application documents, Charpak mandatory documents, and application process steps). This list-based structure is used throughout for clarity.

presentation-quality.requirement: Restates the same eligibility criterion, deadline, or document requirement multiple times without adding new context
presentation-quality.explanation: The response repeats several requirements and deadlines in multiple sections (e.g., INCEPTION/EEF steps, “2 referees,” and Charpak deadline timing) after already stating them earlier, often without adding materially new constraints. It also reiterates the automatic tuition exemption and non-cumulation rules in more than one place with largely the same wording.

presentation-quality.requirement: Uses excessive wordiness, filler phrases, or unnecessary elaboration that extends length without adding substantive information
presentation-quality.explanation: The response is extremely long and includes substantial tangential material (multiple alternative scholarships, tuition policy details, extensive action plans, and repeated “ADVISORY/CRITICAL” notes) beyond the query’s requested admissions and Charpak requirements. It also uses verbose headings and narrative program overviews that add length without directly adding required requirement/deadline/document specifics.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics program pages (such as https://www.universite-paris-saclay.fr/en/education/master/economics) for admission requirements, deadlines, or documents
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites Université Paris-Saclay official Master in Economics pages (e.g., the M1 and M2 Economics pages on universite-paris-saclay.fr) when describing admission requirements, required documents, and application deadlines.

citation-quality.requirement: References Campus France India website (campusfrance.org, https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) for Charpak scholarship eligibility, benefits, or application process
citation-quality.explanation: The response explicitly cites and links to the Campus France India Charpak Master’s Scholarship page (https://www.inde.campusfrance.org/charpak-master-s-scholarship) and uses it to describe eligibility, benefits, documents, and deadlines.

citation-quality.requirement: Cites Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines
citation-quality.explanation: The response cites official Université Paris-Saclay and Campus France pages (plus PDFs) and does not reference Reddit, Quora, student blogs, or third-party scholarship aggregators as primary sources for eligibility criteria or deadlines.
